questionText: A curve is given by $x = 2t$ and $y = 5 - t$ for $0 \le t \le 6$. Find the domain (the values of $x$) and the range (the values of $y$) of the curve.

steps.explanation: Only the values $0 \le t \le 6$ are allowed, so the curve is a segment. Its extreme $x$- and $y$-values are found at the ends of this interval.
Substituting the end values of $t$ into $x = 2t$ gives the smallest and largest $x$-values.
As $t$ increases, $x = 2t$ increases too, so $x$ covers every value from $0$ to $12$.
Substituting the end values of $t$ into $y = 5 - t$ gives the two extreme $y$-values.

steps.description: State the range, watching the direction
steps.explanation: Here $y = 5 - t$ decreases as $t$ increases, so the largest $y$ occurs at $t = 0$ and the smallest at $t = 6$. Writing them in order gives $-1 \le y \le 5$.
These inequalities describe the strip of the plane in which the whole curve lies.
